column1: Tactical Successes and Failures
Argentina’s Proactive Setup: Unlike previous rounds
column3: flooding the midfield and playing through the middle to hurt France

column1: Isolating Di Maria: A key tactical masterstroke was positioning Angel Di Maria on the far left to isolate him 1v1 against Jules Koundé
. This also kept Di Maria as far as possible from Mbappé to prevent quick French transitions
France’s Defensive Weakness: France’s 4-4-2 block struggled because Mbappé does not track back
column2: leaving space between him and Theo Hernandez

column1: . While Antoine Griezmann usually drops deep to cover this
column2: Argentina successfully exploited the gaps in the French midfield

column1: The Defensive Retreat: Argentina's decision to defend their lead by sitting deep was criticized as it played into France's strengths and invited unnecessary pressure onto a "frenetic" backline
Key Parts and Turning Points
Lionel Messi’s Narrative: Messi scored twice and converted his shootout penalty
column2: providing what was described as a "perfect ending" and a "neatness" to his career story

column1: Mbappé’s Resilience: Mbappé became the first player to score a World Cup final hat-trick since 1966
column2: showing immense mental strength to beat goalkeeper Emi Martínez three times from the penalty spot

column1: Emi Martínez’s Heroics: Martínez made a pivotal save in the final seconds of extra time against Kolo Muani
column2: a moment described as one of the saves of the tournament

column1: . His ability to "get in the head" of opponents during the shootout was crucial to the final result
Emotional Resilience: Despite blowing leads at 2-0 and 3-2
